summary_text: Vote: minimal but credible production-like verification plan is below. Required scenarios: 1. `bash tests/test-fugue-codex-implement-kernel-artifacts.sh` to verify wiring in `.github/workflows/fugue-codex-implement.yml` (success gate, `kernel_run_id` gate, and completion-backup invocation contract). 2. `bash tests/test-kernel-run-complete.sh` to verify the wrapper behavior of `scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh` (phase gate + compact artifact update + correct forwarding to backup runner with safe defaults). 3. `bash tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` to verify explicit completion record path in local-safe mode (`--no-gha --dry-run`) and workflow compatibility. 4. `bash tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup-fallback.sh` to verify fallback path when external tooling is absent, including deterministic journal payload and `orchestration_compliance` stamping. Optional scenario: 1. One controlled `repository_dispatch` smoke against a disposable sandbox repo/branch (never production default repo) to validate durable mirror commit path in `kernel-task-completion-backup.yml`. Readiness bar: 1. All required scenarios pass on a clean run, no real dispatch occurs in required runs (`--no-gha --dry-run` enforced), and produced payload/journal fields (`record_id`, `gha_mirror_path`, `orchestration_compliance`, `payload_sha256`) are present and deterministic.
